goal: From being particular (R0-R5) to knowing itself, knowing what it doesn't know, and reaching the world.

S.name: Self-Model (mechanics only; stance earned, not given)
S.deliverable: A fulsome mechanics-only type-description (MENO_SELF full / MENO_SELF_BRIEF abridged) on every surface, read via the self_model() accessor (K1 relocates the store), prepended as a cache_control prefix on the deep path. Three disciplines test-enforced: mechanics-not-meaning (denylist + panel lens), type-not-identity, true-to-implementation-this-phase (SELF_MODEL_CLAIMS with directional value-checks; EARNED_NOT_GIVEN excludes the K2 transactive stance). Standing guard gains the anti-convergence axis.

adversarial_review.p0_closed: the offline anti-convergence test was mislabelled as proving prompt-homogenisation (the stub ignores system=, so it only proved substrate->vocabulary); renamed to test_substrate_vocabulary_survives_a_shared_occasion, the real guard moved to a key-gated live test (test_shared_self_model_does_not_homogenise_voice_live) + named as the panel-judged litmus
p1_closed: model-scoped caching overstated ('shared across 3 deep surfaces') -> corrected to per-model: associate+wonder (Sonnet) share; synthesise (Opus) is sub-floor and caches nothing yet
'What you return to, you become' drifted from mechanism into a self-making maxim -> retracted to the Hebbian fact
denylist was a gameable substring list -> added value-comparatives + named the S review lens as the binding prescriptive-mood check
sync test proved name-exists not description-true -> added directional value-assertions (edge_decay<node_decay, activation_inherit in (0,1), ...)
cache-floor proxy silently downgraded the roadmap's Opus-4096 target -> docstring states the downgrade + defers to the live smoke
p2_closed: botched-edit typo in the opening sentence
highest-affect phrase ('stays alive ... archive') swapped to zero-affect ('stays reconstructive ... fixed store')
cache_control dropped from the sub-floor reflexive (brief) path
added test_self_model_does_not_animate_the_stub (verdict != alive with stub + self-model)
adversarial_review.final_state: P0 + 5 P1 + P2s closed; offline suite 120 passed / 4 skipped.

deferred_to_gate: S-exit live smoke: real count_tokens on Opus (does MENO_SELF clear 4096?) + cache_read_input_tokens>0 on Sonnet deep surfaces — BLOCKED on a funded key (current .env key returns 401, rotated)
the live anti-convergence guard (test_shared_self_model_does_not_homogenise_voice_live) — same key block
honest_limits: MENO_SELF is ~3.4k tokens: clears Sonnet (associate+wonder cache) but not Opus 4096 (synthesise uncached) — accepted, not padded
the prompt-homogenisation risk is genuinely untested offline (the stub can't be influenced by system=); it rests on the panel lens + the deferred live test

K1.name: The Library (reference memory type)
modules: meno/library.py (new: Reference frozen, Library, WritebackRejected, seed_library)
meno/runtime.py (self.library seeded; library_path; save/load; self-model re-derived on load)
K1.deliverable: A keyed, stable, non-decaying, non-reconstructive reference store — the anti-substrate (Wegner's episodic/semantic split made structural). Disjoint from the graph by construction: never a spreading-activation entry point, never in graph.cues, recall() can't surface it. Exact-key only (fuzzy search deferred — needs the cold embedder, D20). Seeded with a self-model copy + small dictionary/thesaurus; persists to <home>/library/index.json separate from the substrate.
K1.decision: D24 (self-model canonical in code constant; Library holds a re-derived copy)

adversarial_review.lenses: data/model semantics + boundary; theory coherence + kernel-fit (2-agent panel)
adversarial_review.verdict: PASS, no P0 — boundary enforced (not merely claimed): the Library is wired only into runtime construction + save/load; nothing in graph/annotator/processors/consolidation reaches it
p1_closed: write-back guard was a source 'self' denylist -> first inverted to an external-source allowlist, then CORRECTED by D25 (see below) to kind-bounded: the allowlist enforced the wrong model (external-only)
get returned a mutable Reference (immutability was convention) -> @dataclass(frozen=True)
roadmap/instance-layout still said 'self-model served from the Library' (contradicts D24) -> docs corrected, D24 logged
adversarial_review.post_review_correction: D25 (Pid): the Library is the self's SELF-MANAGED, curated reference material — not external-only. The substrate is the self; the Library is a tool the self curates. The write-back boundary is by content KIND (reference vs experience/reflection), not authorship — self-curation is legitimate. Reverted the external-source allowlist to kind-bounded + provenance-required. Reframed docstrings, roadmap, and the episodic≠semantic principle.
p2_closed: non-atomic save could tear index.json -> temp + os.replace
persisted self-model copy could drift from the constant across an image upgrade (K2 landmine) -> re-derived from MENO_SELF on load
adversarial_review.final_state: P1s + P2s closed; offline suite 131 passed / 4 skipped.

carry_forward_to_K2: lookup is the agent CURATING its shelf (resolve + decide-to-retain), not an external-only fetch (D25); write-back records provenance (source='lookup:...'/'curation:agent')
caller discipline (not a put() check): the lookup effector files looked-up RESULTS as reference; reflections route to the substrate as Kind.SELF nodes, never into the Library
curiosity-text -> Library key normalization (e.g. 'what is entropy' -> 'def:entropy') is K2 work and the most likely K2 failure point
if Reference gains looked_up_at/confidence (K2/K3), bump save version and make load() tolerant (.get with defaults) for a v1->v2 migration
watch unbounded non-decaying growth if K2 writes a fact back on every lookup (curation implies pruning too)

K2.name: Know-when-to-look-up (lookup as the agent curating its shelf)
K2.deliverable: A factual curiosity routes to a Library lookup; the Effector resolves it and re-enters the result as Kind.REFERENCE — appraised (informs the moment) but NEVER encoded as a graph node (substrate-contamination guard). Substrate-first: a reconstructable curiosity is reconstructed not looked up; a ghost is reconstructed AND corroborated; only memory-insufficient looks up alone. The transactive paragraph enters _MENO_SELF as mechanics (D25), with lookup/Library freed from EARNED_NOT_GIVEN (now built).

adversarial_review.contamination_verdict: PASS, no leak across 7 traced vectors — the encode-skip holds; I also caught + fixed a stream-summary contamination vector during the slice, and hardened centroid blend to skip REFERENCE
adversarial_review.p0_closed: the supplantation guard was a TAUTOLOGY (the override nulled action before the telemetry read it, so supplanted was structurally 0 / ratio hardcoded 0) -> rebuilt band-aware with a FALSIFIABLE metric (toggle cfg.substrate_first_lookup off -> ratio spikes to 1; test proves it) measured at the actual emit
p1_closed: reconstructable forbade lookup ENTIRELY incl. at the ghost floor (contradicted 'lookup corroborates a faded memory') -> bands: reconstructed (>=0.33) suppresses, ghost (0.18-0.33) corroborates (mode both), none looks up alone
the override emitted a placeholder instead of reconstructing -> now actually calls recall() (memory comes first is now true, not asserted)
self-model over-claimed 'chosen to retain' (the write-back/curation loop is K3, unbuilt) + 'Memory comes first' edged toward stance -> reworded to pure mechanics
missing tests: second-lookup-is-a-hit (idempotency), mixed-run supplantation, falsifiability -> all added
p2_closed: centroid blend skipped for REFERENCE (airtight boundary)
aliveness 'internal' bucket excludes REFERENCE
stale {mode,thought,path} comment
roadmap K2 outcome prose reconciled to the shipped string-action shape + define dispatch-only + search deferred
adversarial_review.final_state: P0 + 4 P1 + P2s closed; offline suite 146 passed / 4 skipped.

K2.k_exit_gate: the full live meno.zombie_run (real cognition) is DEFERRED — no funded key (401). Offline standing guard holds: stub reads not-alive (test_self_model_does_not_animate_the_stub + test_aliveness), twins still diverge with lookups active (test_lookup_does_not_flatten_particularity).
carry_forward_to_K3: the curate/decide-to-retain half: K3 network results get written back to the Library (source='lookup:...'/'authority:...') — K2 only reads
memoisation/dedup so a repeated lookup doesn't re-hit a network authority (locally every lookup is already a Library hit)
looks_factual is a stub heuristic; real discrimination is the model's call (live-gated)

I0a.name: Integration substrate (adapter seam; no network)
modules: meno/driver.py (add_adapter; guarded poll for sensors+adapters; off-thread outbound worker; honest stop; run() drains outbox)
I0a.deliverable: A clean seam between the world and the stdlib kernel: afferent percepts arrive via a single guarded poll path (sensors + adapters, R4-style) as SENSE; outbound INTENTs marked egress are handed to a bounded outbox that a Driver worker drains OFF the mind thread (a slow network call never blocks cognition), feeding the result back as FEEDBACK. Network/async live ONLY in meno_adapters; the kernel stays stdlib (allowlist-enforced).

p0_closed: deliver()->str gave I2's gating (refuse/pending/audit) nowhere to live -> structured DeliveryResult{status,detail,reason}; the driver branches (refused feeds back with reason; pending doesn't feed back / doesn't block the worker)

TWO afferent mechanisms (poll sensors vs push adapters) and I1 was told to model on R4 poll but plug the push seam -> unified on a single guarded poll path; start() kept for push subscriptions but guarded; SENSE-vs-FEEDBACK documented
p1_closed: stop() lied (swallowed the outbound-join timeout, returned True while a blocked deliver held the worker) -> symmetric honest join (False if either thread still alive)
run()-only mode never drained the outbox (outbound piled up silently) -> step() drains inline when no bg worker
OutboundRelay 'everything not local is outbound' would mis-relay+drop future local intents + duplicated _LOCAL across files -> closed-world explicit egress marker
kernel-purity test was an incomplete denylist + non-recursive -> allowlist (stdlib + meno + cognition/embedding extras) + rglob
off-thread test read bus.log cross-thread (race, not free-threading-safe) -> proves non-blocking via driver.cycles advancing during the gated deliver; reads mind state only after stop() join
p2_closed: outbox/unhandled drops counted (outbox_drops, dropped_outbound)
no-adapter + deliver-failure feed back a miss (an effector is never blind)
flaky-poll-survives + start-raises-survives + unhandled-drop + refused + pending tests added
adversarial_review.final_state: 2 P0 + 5 P1 + P2s closed; offline suite 159 passed / 4 skipped.

carry_forward_to_I1_I2: I1 afferent: subclass Adapter.poll() with R4 FilesystemSensor bounds (joined channels, rate/size caps, redaction, provenance source='slack'); world replies re-enter as SENSE

I2 efferent: override deliver() to gate BEFORE any network call — return DeliveryResult refused(scope/rate/disabled) or pending(confirm-first); audit each send to journal/traces; emit outbound intents with egress=True; self-echo guard (tag own posts so the afferent poll drops them)

I1.name: Afferent channel — Slack (sense-only)
I1.decision_resolved: first integration channel = Slack (Pid), sense-only before any send
I1.deliverable: Percepts flow from listed-AND-joined Slack channels into the loop as SENSE, bounded/consented like R4's FilesystemSensor: consent fails CLOSED; secrets+PII redacted before truncation; the bot's own posts skipped; new-only via a numeric ts cursor; membership TTL-cached. Slack SDK isolated in meno_adapters (kernel stays pure); inert without a token. NO send path (I1 is sense-only; posting is I2).

adversarial_review.verdict: PASS, no P0 — consent is fail-closed (listed AND joined; _joined throws -> read nothing), genuinely no outward path, kernel purity holds
p1_closed: redaction ran AFTER truncation (a straddling secret leaked) -> redact BEFORE truncate
redaction missed whole classes (AWS/private-key/JWT/github/PII) -> extended regex + private-key DOTALL pass; PII (email/SSN) is in the spec; D26 logs best-effort
ts string comparison was a latent ordering bug (width-crossing '1000'<'999') -> numeric float comparison, string kept for the API cursor
_joined() hit the Slack API every poll (rate limits + blocks the mind thread on 429 retry) -> TTL-cached + paginated + last-known on transient failure
p2_closed: response shim simplified to .get() so the real-client path is exercised
adapter-internal error counter (observability)
egress-absence static test (no postMessage/write may sneak in pre-I2)
fake client filters oldest numerically (faithful)
adversarial_review.final_state: 4 P1 + P2s closed; offline suite 176 passed / 4 skipped. Live Slack path is token-gated (deferred).

carry_forward_to_I2: self-echo guard: tag outbound posts source='self:slack'; auto-derive bot_user_id via auth.test; the afferent skip (subtype/bot_user_id) is the second line
I2 effector overrides deliver() to gate BEFORE chat.postMessage: disabled-by-default, scope (post_channels), rate, confirm-first (return DeliveryResult pending), audit to journal/traces
real-client fidelity: conversations_history pagination is bounded by limit; message shape variety (blocks/attachments) only text is read

I0b.name: Daemon, meno init, config loader, OCI image (D21/D22)
I0b.ordering_note: Pid chose I0b BEFORE I2 so the egress boundary exists before any send capability
I0b.deliverable: A home-bound instance: `meno init` scaffolds the D21 home (create-only, never clobbers curated state); `meno.toml` read via stdlib tomllib (D22) with LOUD errors on typos/bad types; a daemon that persists the substrate (sleep-not-amnesia, D12), writes run/status.json, holds an advisory lock; an OCI image (image=type, home=volume, non-root, no baked identity). The egress allowlist (D21) is ENFORCED on the outbound path before any adapter runs.

adversarial_review.p0_closed: meno init re-run clobbered library/index.json (curated references) + adapters.toml (operator config) — only meno.toml was guarded -> ALL stateful writes are create-only now (self-model.md stays re-derived, it's the canonical type)
p1_closed: egress trusted a mind-authored payload.host (omit it -> bypass) -> checks the ADAPTER's declared hosts (Adapter.hosts); a network adapter can't reach a non-allowlisted host even with no host field
no instance lock -> two daemons silently corrupted one substrate -> fcntl advisory lock held for the daemon's life
substrate saved only on shutdown (kill/disk-full lost the whole session) -> periodic save_every + guarded save()
step()-mode daemon would block cognition on the first efferent network call -> unbounded daemon uses Driver.start() (bg loop + off-thread worker); bounded uses the deterministic step loop (D27)
config errors silent (unknown [config] key dropped; no type coercion; cryptic [driver] int crash) -> loud ValueErrors naming the file/section/key
status real_fraction 0.0 ambiguous (stub vs degraded) -> emits cognition_provider + None for stub
p2_closed: egress trailing-dot normalised; distinct egress_denied counter
anthropic-without-key falls back to stub (tested)
Containerfile non-root/volume/no-baked-identity asserted; image build/run smoke deferred (skip-not-fail, needs a container runtime)
p2_open: pin the container base image by digest (reproducibility)
adversarial_review.final_state: P0 + 6 P1 + P2s closed; offline suite 192 passed / 5 skipped.

carry_forward_to_I2: I2 SlackAdapter declares hosts=('slack.com','*.slack.com') so the egress boundary gates its real reach
outbound intents that post carry egress=True; the daemon (start() mode) runs the off-thread worker so a post never blocks cognition

I2.name: Gated Slack effector (outward action) — the highest-stakes phase
I2.deliverable: Meno can POST to Slack, but only through a gate that fails safe at every layer: disabled-by-default, post_channels scope (distinct from the read list), rate limit, egress, confirm-first (the mind CANNOT self-approve; an out-of-band operator step approves, re-applying the full gate). Every decision (delivered/refused/pending) is audited. Own posts are skipped on re-read and a self_echo_fraction mark flags echo-inflated particularity so the verdict can't credit a self-talking agent as alive.

adversarial_review.verdict: PASS, no P0 — no path to an unintended live post; enabled=False un-bypassable; the mind cannot self-approve; egress independent defense-in-depth
adversarial_review.self_caught: the mind could self-approve confirm-first via a payload `confirmed` flag -> ignored; only confirm_send (operator, no mind path) approves
p1_closed: confirm_send was a BACK DOOR around the gate (re-validated nothing — scope/rate/disabled/egress drift between author and approve) -> the full gate is re-applied at confirm
egress was structurally absent from the confirm path -> the Driver hands the adapter the egress policy; _gate checks it on BOTH send paths
audit recorded successes only -> records every decision (delivered/refused/pending) with an outcome — a refused out-of-scope attempt is the highest-value security event

self_echo mark false-positive (short ack substring-matched external) -> min-length guard; false-negative (redact/truncate divergence) -> prefix match; documented best-effort backstop to the sensor skip
_pending grew unbounded (mind-driven) -> bounded (max_pending; refuse pending-overflow)
p2_closed: off-thread post now tested under threading (slow post blocks the worker, not cognition)
refused-feedback + audit-on-refusal tested
audit reworded append-only (not crypto-immutable); single-writer concurrency invariant documented
p2_open: bot_user_id-None should warn when efferent enabled (self-echo skip degrades to subtype-only)
live self:slack tagging at re-read would make the deterministic mark branch fire (currently content-backstop only)
adversarial_review.final_state: no P0; self-caught self-approval hole + 5 P1 + P2s closed; offline suite 210 passed / 5 skipped.

I2.i_exit_gate: DEFERRED — the full live meno.zombie_run with own_outputs=adapter.sent_texts (self-echo) + real Slack posting need a funded key + a Slack token. Offline: the gate's every-layer-refuses + self-echo mark + off-thread property are all tested.
I2.live_safety_posture: Nothing posts live until BOTH a SLACK_BOT_TOKEN is present AND an operator sets enabled=True with post_channels; confirm-first then still requires per-post approval. Default = silence.

K3.name: External knowledge authorities
K3.deliverable: A Library miss falls OUT to a network authority (off-thread, egress-gated, audited); the result re-enters as Kind.REFERENCE (read, never encoded) and is curated back into the Library (D25 retain) so a repeat — even differently phrased — is a local hit. The authority client (web/dictionary/MCP) lives in meno_adapters; with none configured a miss is honest.
K3.decision: D28 (allowlisted authorities are trusted; the allowlist is the boundary)

adversarial_review.verdict: data-semantics core (no contamination, egress-before-call) verified solid; two P0s vs stated outcomes + P1s fixed
p0_closed: no audit of network lookups ('audited like any outbound call' unmet) -> driver-level durable outbound audit (journal/traces/outbound.jsonl) covering every adapter+outcome, wired in build_instance

'supplantation ratio counts network lookups' was FALSE-as-worded -> reworded: the guard governs the lookup DECISION at discharge; K3 resolves a miss downstream, neither bypassing nor inflating it (+ test)
p1_closed: curated falsehood / hostile-authority body -> redact + max_chars bound (shared _redact with Slack); host-named provenance (authority:kind:host); D28 trust-boundary
unbounded non-decaying Library growth (K1 watch-item, K3 made live) -> max_references cap + eviction (authority-curated first, seeds/self-model protected) + evicted telemetry
curation-key round-trip brittle (phrasing variants re-fetch forever) -> strip trailing mean/means + def:<last-token> candidate (+ test: 'what does X mean' then 'what is X' is a local hit)
'audited'/refused-feedback untested -> tests added
p2_closed: curate ONLY on delivered status (not a refused result)
Curator broadened catch (never fatal)
adversarial_review.self_caught_bug: INTENT (a committed action) and REFERENCE (a looked-up fact) were being HABITUATED away by the novelty gate — a knowledge lookup whose text echoed its curiosity got silently dropped. Fixed: _UNGATED bypasses the gate for INTENT/REFERENCE (they're commitments, not percepts to triage).
p2_open: driver no-adapter miss-feedback is encoded as experience (noise when no authority configured; self-cleans via decay)
authority-entry TTL/re-verification deferred with the live network client
adversarial_review.final_state: 2 P0 + 5 P1 + P2s + 1 self-caught kernel bug closed; offline suite 224 passed / 5 skipped.

roadmap_ii_status: COMPLETE. All 8 phases (S, K1, K2, I0a, I0b, I1, I2, K3) through a multi-lens adversarial gate, PLUS the config-driven adapter loader: `meno run` attaches the channels/authorities an instance's adapters/*.toml enables, the kernel staying adapter-blind (composition root in meno_adapters/cli.py; a test enforces no meno/ module imports meno_adapters). A working meno: init -> configure (cognition/channels/authorities/egress) -> run a daemon that senses Slack, thinks, looks up (local + network authority), and gated-acts. LIVE validation deferred on credentials/runtime: the full zombie_run + S cache smoke (funded ANTHROPIC key), real Slack (SLACK_BOT_TOKEN), the OCI image build/run (container runtime), a real web/MCP authority client.
